summary: DEIMoS functionality includes feature detection, feature alignment, collision cross section calibration, isotope detection, and MS/MS spectral deconvolution.
DEIMoS algorithm implementations increase alignment and feature matching confidence among datasets through simultaneous use of all dimensions.
Alignment is the process by which feature coordinates across samples are adjusted to account for instrument variation such that matching features are aligned.
DEIMoS implements reference-based alignment by constructing a model for each dimension of a sample by matching detected features against a reference sample.
Cross-sample alignment involves matching corresponding features across data sets within a user defined tolerance.
